what: Executive-Grade Themes and Helpers for ggplot2
title: Executive-Grade Themes and Helpers for ggplot2
text: opinionated ggplot2 extension executive-grade consulting output. Ships archetype themes, palettes, mechanical geom wrappers, (later releases) locale-aware label helpers data-aware polish layer. Heads : ggconsulting early development. public API shaped real consulting decks; expect breaking changes 0.x.

title: Executive-Grade Themes and Helpers for ggplot2
text: development version GitHub:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("viniciusoike/ggconsulting")

title: Executive-Grade Themes and Helpers for ggplot2
text: theme_strategy() routes palette’s main colour ggplot2 4.x’s from_theme() mechanism, unmapped geoms inherit automatically — scale_color_*() calls needed single-series case. ggconsulting also sets small set aesthetic defaults library() attach (currently geom_point size = 2.5). Opt via:
code: library(ggplot2) #> Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.2 library(ggconsulting) #> v ggconsulting set ggplot2 aesthetic defaults #> i Opt out: `ct_unset_defaults()` or `options(ggconsulting.autoload = FALSE)` #> i Column width / linewidth: use `ct_col()` / `ct_line()`, or apply #>   `ct_theme()`/`theme_strategy()` for linewidth via `from_theme()`  ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   labs(     title    = "Fuel efficiency vs. vehicle weight",     subtitle = "1974 Motor Trend data",     caption  = "Source: datasets::mtcars"   ) +   theme_strategy() options(ggconsulting.autoload = FALSE) # or, mid-session: ct_unset_defaults()

title: Executive-Grade Themes and Helpers for ggplot2
text: ct_theme() / theme_strategy() — composable theme builder archetype preset. Built ggplot2 4.x theme_sub_*() helpers routed element_geom() from_theme() linkage. Five starter palettes — strategy_navy, strategy_emerald, strategy_crimson, strategy_azure, strategy_slate. ct_col() / ct_line() / ct_point() — mechanical wrappers consulting-grade defaults call-site overrides. ct_set_defaults() / ct_unset_defaults() — update_geom_defaults()-driven aesthetic baseline honest revert.

title: Executive-Grade Themes and Helpers for ggplot2
text: Next prompts add theme_finance() / theme_editorial() archetypes, scale_color_ct() / scale_fill_ct(), ct_finish() polish layer, locale-aware label helpers (fmt_brl, ct_locale), install_consulting_fonts(), vignettes, polished gallery.

what: Set or restore ggconsulting aesthetic defaults — ct_defaults
title: Set or restore ggconsulting aesthetic defaults — ct_defaults
text: ct_set_defaults() overrides ggplot2 aesthetic defaults via ggplot2::update_geom_defaults(). v0.1 sets single override: geom_point size = 2.5. ct_unset_defaults() restores whatever values active first time ct_set_defaults() ran — ggplot2's untouched baseline. Originals captured package-private environment, repeated ct_set_defaults() calls idempotent. Column width linewidth handled elsewhere — see ct_col() / ct_line() explicit formal overrides, apply ct_theme() / theme_strategy() linewidth via from_theme().

title: Set or restore ggconsulting aesthetic defaults — ct_defaults
code: ct_set_defaults()  ct_unset_defaults()

title: Set or restore ggconsulting aesthetic defaults — ct_defaults
text: functions return invisible(NULL).

title: Set or restore ggconsulting aesthetic defaults — ct_defaults
code: ct_set_defaults() ct_unset_defaults()

what: Apply data-aware finishing touches to a plot — ct_finish
title: Apply data-aware finishing touches to a plot — ct_finish
text: Companion ct_theme() runs geom layer built, can inspect data + active geom inject value labels, sorting, highlighting, end labels, scale expansion. Compose +, geoms.

title: Apply data-aware finishing touches to a plot — ct_finish
code: ct_finish(   values = FALSE,   sort = NULL,   label_fmt = NULL,   highlight = NULL,   end_labels = FALSE,   expand = "auto",   muted_color = "#A8A4A0" )

title: Apply data-aware finishing touches to a plot — ct_finish
text: values TRUE adds value labels bars / next points. "auto" adds first geom column bar. sort One "asc", "desc", NULL. set, reorders factor levels x aesthetic y aesthetic. label_fmt Either formatter function (anything maps numeric vector character vector), one shortcut names "brl", "number", "pct", "delta" resolved matching fmt_*() helper. highlight Value(s) x aesthetic emphasise. Matching bars use active palette's main colour; non-matching bars use muted_color. Inserted scale_*_manual(). end_labels line plots: TRUE, label last point series group identifier. expand "auto" picks geom-aware scale expansion (room column tops, right-side room line end labels); FALSE disables. muted_color Fill / colour used non-highlighted categories.

title: Apply data-aware finishing touches to a plot — ct_finish
text: ct_finish object, added plot via +. ggplot_add() method composes requested layers scales.

title: Apply data-aware finishing touches to a plot — ct_finish
code: library(ggplot2) d <- data.frame(g = LETTERS[1:5], v = c(3, 8, 5, 12, 7)) p <- ggplot(d, aes(g, v)) +   geom_col() +   ct_finish(values = TRUE, sort = "desc", label_fmt = "brl", highlight = "D") +   theme_strategy()

title: Locale-aware label formatters — ct_formatters
text: Factory functions: returns function compatible scales::label_* (callable numeric vector). Marks (thousands, decimals) currency symbols come active ggconsulting locale, set via ct_locale(). fmt_number() — plain numbers locale separators. fmt_brl() — Brazilian Real. Always renders R$ regardless active locale; locale argument lets override marks. fmt_currency() — uses active locale's currency symbol. fmt_pct() — percentages, interpreting input fraction (0.5 → "50%"). fmt_delta() — percentage-point-style signed deltas (+1,2pp / -0,3pp / 0,0pp). fmt_month() — Date / POSIXct → localised month string. Negatives default minus prefix; currency helpers also support style = "accounting", wraps negatives parentheses. Currency rendering uses non-breaking space (U+00A0) symbol number pair never wraps across lines.

title: Locale-aware label formatters — ct_formatters
code: fmt_number(decimals = 0, locale = NULL)  fmt_brl(decimals = 2, style = c("minus", "accounting"), locale = NULL)  fmt_currency(decimals = 2, style = c("minus", "accounting"), locale = NULL)  fmt_pct(decimals = 1, locale = NULL, accuracy = NULL)  fmt_delta(decimals = 1, suffix = "pp", locale = NULL)  fmt_month(format = c("abbr", "full"), locale = NULL)

title: Locale-aware label formatters — ct_formatters
text: decimals Number decimal places. locale Optional locale name. Defaults active locale. style "minus" (default) "accounting" currency. accuracy Optional explicit accuracy passed scales::percent(); overrides decimals set. suffix Suffix fmt_delta(). Defaults "pp". format "abbr" (default) "full" fmt_month().

title: Locale-aware label formatters — ct_formatters
text: formatter function: function(x) character.

title: Locale-aware label formatters — ct_formatters

what: Drop-in replacements for geom_col(), geom_line(), and geom_point() — ct_geoms
title: Drop-in replacements for geom_col(), geom_line(), and geom_point() — ct_geoms
text: Three-line pass-throughs ship consulting-grade defaults ggplot2::geom_col() width, ggplot2::geom_line() linewidth, ggplot2::geom_point() size. Use want defaults baked call site; plain geom_line() also pick linewidth ct_theme() / theme_strategy() via from_theme(), geom_point() size autoloaded ct_set_defaults().

title: Drop-in replacements for geom_col(), geom_line(), and geom_point() — ct_geoms
code: ct_col(..., width = 0.8)  ct_line(..., linewidth = 0.7)  ct_point(..., size = 2.5)

title: Drop-in replacements for geom_col(), geom_line(), and geom_point() — ct_geoms
text: ... Forwarded underlying ggplot2 constructor. width Column width. Defaults 0.8 (vs ggplot2's 0.9). linewidth Line width. Defaults 0.7 (vs ggplot2's 0.5). size Point size. Defaults 2.5 (vs ggplot2's 1.5).

title: Drop-in replacements for geom_col(), geom_line(), and geom_point() — ct_geoms
text: ggplot2 ggplot2::layer() object.

title: Drop-in replacements for geom_col(), geom_line(), and geom_point() — ct_geoms
code: library(ggplot2) d <- data.frame(g = c("A", "B", "C"), v = c(3, 5, 2)) p_col   <- ggplot(d, aes(g, v)) + ct_col() p_line  <- ggplot(economics, aes(date, unemploy)) + ct_line() p_point <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + ct_point()

what: Set the active ggconsulting locale — ct_locale
title: Set the active ggconsulting locale — ct_locale
text: Stores choice options(ggconsulting.locale = ...). active locale read formatter helpers (fmt_number(), fmt_currency(), fmt_month(), etc.) locale argument NULL. Defaults "pt-BR"; "en-US" always available.

title: Set the active ggconsulting locale — ct_locale
code: ct_locale(locale = c("pt-BR", "en-US"))

title: Set the active ggconsulting locale — ct_locale
text: locale One "pt-BR" "en-US".

title: Set the active ggconsulting locale — ct_locale
text: previous locale, invisibly. Use round-trip: old <- ct_locale("en-US"); ...; ct_locale(old).

title: Set the active ggconsulting locale — ct_locale
text: touch Sys.setlocale() — OS-dependent breaks Windows CI. ggconsulting ships month tables formatting marks instead.

title: Set the active ggconsulting locale — ct_locale
code: old <- ct_locale("en-US") ct_locale(old)

what: Preview ggconsulting palettes as a swatch — ct_palette_show
title: Preview ggconsulting palettes as a swatch — ct_palette_show
text: Returns ggplot showing palettes colour tiles hex value overlaid monospace. palette = NULL, every palette shipped .ct_palettes shown faceted.

title: Preview ggconsulting palettes as a swatch — ct_palette_show
code: ct_palette_show(palette = NULL)

title: Preview ggconsulting palettes as a swatch — ct_palette_show
text: palette Palette name (e.g. "strategy_emerald"), character vector colours, NULL (default) show every shipped palette.

title: Preview ggconsulting palettes as a swatch — ct_palette_show
text: ggplot object.

title: Preview ggconsulting palettes as a swatch — ct_palette_show
code: p_all  <- ct_palette_show() p_one  <- ct_palette_show("strategy_emerald") p_vec  <- ct_palette_show(c("#0F4D38", "#177B57", "#3DA876"))

what: Consulting colour and fill scales — ct_scales
title: Consulting colour and fill scales — ct_scales
text: Discrete continuous scales backed ggconsulting palettes. Discrete variants interpolate (warning) data levels palette can hold. Continuous variants gradient across palette colours via ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn() / ggplot2::scale_fill_gradientn(). British spellings (scale_colour_ct(), scale_colour_ct_c()) provided aliases.

title: Consulting colour and fill scales — ct_scales
code: scale_color_ct(palette = "strategy_navy", reverse = FALSE, ...)  scale_colour_ct(palette = "strategy_navy", reverse = FALSE, ...)  scale_fill_ct(palette = "strategy_navy", reverse = FALSE, ...)  scale_color_ct_c(palette = "strategy_navy", direction = 1, ...)  scale_colour_ct_c(palette = "strategy_navy", direction = 1, ...)  scale_fill_ct_c(palette = "strategy_navy", direction = 1, ...)

title: Consulting colour and fill scales — ct_scales
text: palette Palette name (e.g. "strategy_navy") character vector colours. reverse Reverse palette order mapping. Defaults FALSE. ... Forwarded underlying ggplot2 scale constructor. direction 1 (default) -1 reverse continuous gradient.

title: Consulting colour and fill scales — ct_scales
text: ggplot2 scale.

title: Consulting colour and fill scales — ct_scales
code: library(ggplot2) p_d <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point() +   scale_color_ct("strategy_azure") p_c <- ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +   geom_raster() +   scale_fill_ct_c("strategy_emerald")

what: Build a consulting-grade ggplot2 theme — ct_theme
title: Build a consulting-grade ggplot2 theme — ct_theme
text: Flexible builder behind archetype presets (theme_strategy() friends). Composes ggplot2::theme_minimal() executive-output overrides via theme_sub_*() helpers, sets theme geom element from_theme()-aware layers (e.g. ggplot2::geom_line()) inherit resolved linewidth main colour, applies resolved font fallback chain.

title: Build a consulting-grade ggplot2 theme — ct_theme
code: ct_theme(   palette = "strategy_navy",   font = "Inter",   font_fallback = c("Helvetica Neue", "Arial", "sans"),   density = c("normal", "tight", "loose"),   context = c("presentation", "report", "screen"),   base_size = NULL,   main_color = NULL )

title: Build a consulting-grade ggplot2 theme — ct_theme
text: palette Palette name (e.g. "strategy_navy") character vector colours. font Preferred font family. font_fallback Character vector fallback families try font unavailable. generic R families "sans", "serif", "mono" always recognised terminal fallbacks. density One "normal", "tight", "loose". Controls panel.spacing axis-text margins. context One "presentation", "report", "screen". Drives default base_size plot.margin. base_size Base font size. NULL, derived context. main_color Colour used plot title routed theme geom element's ink slot, unmapped geoms pick via from_theme(). NULL falls back palette's first colour.

title: Build a consulting-grade ggplot2 theme — ct_theme
text: ggplot2::theme() object.

title: Build a consulting-grade ggplot2 theme — ct_theme
code: library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   ct_theme()

what: ggconsulting: Executive-Grade Themes and Helpers for 'ggplot2' — ggconsulting-package

title: ggconsulting: Executive-Grade Themes and Helpers for 'ggplot2' — ggconsulting-package
text: Maintainer: Vinicius Reginatto viniciusoike@gmail.com

title: Editorial archetype theme — theme_editorial
text: Preset path ct_theme() tuned analyst notes market commentary: serif typography ("Source Serif 4" Georgia / Times / serif fallback chain), slightly larger title tighter leading, italic subtitles typographic personality.

title: Editorial archetype theme — theme_editorial
code: theme_editorial(main_color = NULL, ...)

title: Editorial archetype theme — theme_editorial
text: main_color Colour title theme geom ink slot. NULL (default) falls back editorial_warm[1]. ... Forwarded ct_theme() — e.g. density, context, base_size, explicit palette override.

title: Editorial archetype theme — theme_editorial
text: ggplot2::theme() object.

title: Editorial archetype theme — theme_editorial
code: library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(economics, aes(date, unemploy)) +   geom_line() +   theme_editorial()

title: Finance archetype theme — theme_finance
text: Preset path ct_theme() tuned finance reports pitch books: serif typography ("Source Serif 4" Georgia / Times / serif fallback chain), regular-weight title (restrained — finance reports avoid bold), lighter major gridline strategy archetype.

title: Finance archetype theme — theme_finance
code: theme_finance(main_color = NULL, ...)

title: Finance archetype theme — theme_finance
text: main_color Colour title theme geom ink slot. NULL (default) falls back finance_classic[1]. ... Forwarded ct_theme() — e.g. density, context, base_size, explicit palette override.

title: Finance archetype theme — theme_finance
text: ggplot2::theme() object.

title: Finance archetype theme — theme_finance
code: library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(economics, aes(date, unemploy)) +   geom_line() +   theme_finance()

title: Strategy archetype theme — theme_strategy
text: Preset path ct_theme() tuned minimal, generous-whitespace, navy-default look inspired top-tier global strategy consultancies.

title: Strategy archetype theme — theme_strategy
code: theme_strategy(main_color = NULL, ...)

title: Strategy archetype theme — theme_strategy
text: main_color Colour used plot title. Defaults strategy_navy palette's primary title matches downstream data marks. Pass value grDevices::col2rgb() accepts override. ... Forwarded ct_theme() — e.g. density, context, base_size.

title: Strategy archetype theme — theme_strategy
text: ggplot2::theme() object.

title: Strategy archetype theme — theme_strategy
code: library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   theme_strategy()

title: ggconsulting (development version)
text: ct_theme() builder composed via theme_sub_*() helpers routed element_geom() from_theme()-aware geoms inherit palette’s main colour (ink) default linewidth. theme_strategy() archetype preset path. Five starter palettes: strategy_navy, strategy_emerald, strategy_crimson, strategy_azure, strategy_slate. ct_col() / ct_line() / ct_point() mechanical wrappers explicit-override use call site. ct_set_defaults() / ct_unset_defaults() true aesthetic defaults (currently geom_point size = 2.5), autoloaded library attach opt-option. has_font() internal helper.

title: ggconsulting (development version)
text: R-CMD-check workflow across macOS, Windows, Ubuntu (R release) Ubuntu (R devel). test-coverage workflow via covr + Codecov v4. README badges: R-CMD-check status, Codecov coverage, License: MIT.

title: ggconsulting (development version)
text: scale_color_ct() / scale_fill_ct() — discrete scales backed ggconsulting palettes; interpolate emit cli::cli_warn() n exceeds palette size. scale_color_ct_c() / scale_fill_ct_c() — continuous variants via ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn() / scale_fill_gradientn(). British-spelling aliases: scale_colour_ct(), scale_colour_ct_c(). ct_palette_show() — swatch preview single palette, custom hex vector, every shipped palette faceted.

title: ggconsulting (development version)
text: theme_finance() — serif preset (Source Serif 4 → Georgia → Times New Roman → serif) regular-weight title lighter major gridline. Defaults finance_classic palette. theme_editorial() — serif preset italic subtitle slightly larger, tighter-leaded title. Defaults editorial_warm palette. ct_theme() gained font_fallback argument; .resolve_font() now recognises "sans", "serif", "mono" guaranteed terminal fallbacks. Six new palettes: finance_classic, finance_steel, finance_burgundy, editorial_warm, editorial_clay, editorial_oxide. catalog now 11 palettes (5 strategy + 3 finance + 3 editorial).

title: ggconsulting (development version)
text: ct_locale("pt-BR" | "en-US") — session-scoped locale switch stored options(ggconsulting.locale). touch Sys.setlocale(); portable across Windows / Linux / macOS CI. fmt_number() — locale-aware number formatter (1.234,5 / 1,234.5). fmt_brl() — Brazilian Real formatter; always renders R$ non-breaking space, regardless active locale. Supports style = "accounting" parens-wrapped negatives. fmt_currency() — uses active locale’s currency symbol. fmt_pct() — fraction--percent (0.5 → "50%"). fmt_delta() — always-signed percentage-point-style deltas (+1,2pp / -0,3pp / 0,0pp). fmt_month() — Date / POSIXct → localised month string; ships pt-BR en-US month tables (LC_TIME reliance).

title: ggconsulting (development version)
text: inject value labels bars / next points (values = TRUE "auto") reorder categorical x y (sort = "asc" | "desc") format labels via shortcut names (label_fmt = "brl" | "number" | "pct" | "delta") user-supplied function highlight specific x values theme’s main colour mute rest muted_color (default #A8A4A0, warm-leaning neutral reads cool warm palettes) label last point line series (end_labels = TRUE) geom-aware scale expansion (expand = "auto") — y room columns, right-side room line endpoints
